acceptance.proof: The new entry point admits one caller-explicit concrete IPv4 interface for the exact group while unspecified, multicast, and broadcast interface values reject before I/O.
The existing loopback entry point, capability activation, query/response, deadline, cancellation, and cleanup owners remain passing.
Focused damaged checks, owner/public API tests, Standard, public boundary, instructions, and workflow pass without selection or authority widening.

commit_policy: Commit lifecycle separately; keep production change limited to explicit concrete-interface admission and delegation into the accepted bounded responder; keep private interface/run details outside the repository.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Bound the exact concrete-interface responder generalization and nonclaims.
Route the explicit-interface runtime surface without implying selection policy.
Record concrete-interface validation and preserved loopback wrapper.
Bind LSLC-004H evidence to the bounded implementation and limitations.
Route focused, damaged, owner, Standard, and workflow gates.

non_scope: Interface enumeration, default-interface selection, unspecified-address fallback, ambient fallback, IPv6, other groups or ports, multiple requests, retries or background workers, requester changes, activation changes, dependencies, devices, unsafe/FFI, routing, admission, commands, broad compatibility, or Manifold authority.
objective: Generalize only the exact-group capability-gated responder from a caller-explicit loopback IPv4 interface to one caller-explicit concrete IPv4 interface, preserving the existing loopback entry point, exact 239.255.172.215:16571 destination, one bounded query/response, deadline, cancellation, cleanup, inert activation, and authority boundaries.
outputs: One capability-gated exact-group responder entry point requiring a caller-explicit concrete IPv4 interface.
Preserved loopback wrapper and finite owner behavior with typed non-concrete rejection.
Focused fixture/checker, damaged cases, documentation, policy, and private active-interface composition evidence.
